programme.title: Taboo Photos Murder Trial
programme.desc: The Defense gave its opening statement to jurors. Jade Janks lead attorney dismissed the State's assertion that she plotted the murder of her stepfather, Tom Merriman. Instead, the Defense argued that he died of drug abuse. No evidence of strangulation and plenty of evidence of drug abuse. The Defense wanted to raise doubt in the prosecution's case against her.

programme.title: Taboo Photos Murder Trial
programme.desc: Prosecutors wanted to show how far they believed the former interior designer went to kill her stepfather, Tom Merriman, and dispose of his body. They called to the stand one of the men she was accused of asking to help her. Brian Salomon claimed that Jade Janks wanted to put a pillowcase over her stepfather's head and a rope around his neck. Salomon testified that he ran away from her at one point on the day prosecutors said she killed her stepfather.

programme.title: Taboo Photos Murder Trial
programme.desc: The Defense tried to discredit the man who claimed he was asked to help her kill her stepdad, Tom Merriman. On the stand, Brian Salomon admitted that he did not go to police after his encounter with the defendant because he was afraid it would incriminate him. Jurors would decide later what was the actual involvement of the man who Jade Janks allegedly tried to recruit in a murder plot. Was he an accomplice and culprit in Merriman's death?

programme.title: Taboo Photos Murder Trial
programme.desc: The man who found himself in the middle of an alleged murder plot wrapped up his testimony.

programme.title: Taboo Photos Murder Trial
programme.desc: A detective who worked the investigation into Tom Merriman's death collected a number of items linked to this case which included a black glove, nylon cords, a towel and ropes. Jade Janks told detectives that she bought the items at a nearby pharmacy for a project. But authorities believed she had a more sinister use for them, murder. Next, a neighbor of the victim told the jury that the last time he saw Merriman alive was the day before police say he was killed.

programme.desc: In 1990, a 14-year-old girl ran away from her Texas home and disappeared; six months later, her body was found hundreds of miles away in an Illinois barn. Agents tracked down a trucker who they believed was responsible for over 50 murders.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: After two brothers abduct and murder bank president Dan Short, FBI agents slowly piece together tiny shreds of evidence. Eventually, they're able to convict the Agofsky brothers for the 1989 Noel, Missouri bank robbery.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1993, a mother, her 8-year-old son and a live-in nurse were brutally murdered by an assassin. The murder-for-hire plot was meticulously planned and executed.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: The body of an unidentified young woman turned up on a highway in a small Pennsylvania town. Agents persisted, eventually identifying the body and finding the killer.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: New Jersey millionaire Frank Black flew to West Palm Beach, Florida to close a major business deal but never returned home. When the FBI was called in, agents learned of a mystery woman whom Black met while on his trip.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A teenager was shot to death on a hunting ground in Virginia. His death was initially considered an accident but investigators soon suspected foul play. Years later, a man contacted the FBI with a complex story of conspiracy and murder-for-hire.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: When authorities in a small Washington town began investigating the disappearance of a young woman, they had no idea it would turn into a nationwide hunt for a sadistic sexual predator. The FBI was called in to help track down Darren Dee O'Neall.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Six Black men had been brutally murdered; two had their hearts cut out. The only thing connecting the victims was their ethnicity and the fact that each had been shot with .22 caliber bullets.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A class field trip ended with an 11-year-old girl missing. When the parents failed to locate their daughter in their quiet Illinois town, the FBI joined the investigation, working with friends and family in an urgent search to find the missing girl.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In New York, an armored car robbery left one guard dead and another critically wounded. The FBI suspected domestic terrorists with a history of violent crime. Their investigation led them to the Black Liberation Army and the Weather Underground.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Police in Aurora, Colorado found the body of a young man in in his own home with a bullet wound to the head.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Feared L.A. crime boss Ezequiel Romo ruthlessly runs the streets of L.A. - even from behind bars. Leading the Blythe Street gang from his cell, he kills with a single phone call, coldly ordering the murders of anyone who stands in his way.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: The story of Tyler Davis Bingham, pretty criminal turned leader of the brutal and feared Aryan Brotherhood.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Feared Aryan Warriors gang leader 'Mugsy' Williams was a one-man crimewave. His violence ultimately sparked a bloody showdown in a Nevada jail, leaving multiple inmates dead.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Edward Johnson is sent to prison for the brutal murder of a female friend, but the violence doesn't end there.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Sarah Williams and Katrina Walsh: the story of two friends who carried out the evil, jealousy-fuelled murder of Sadie Hartley.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Jordan Monaghan killed his 24-day-old daughter Ruby on New Year's Day in January 2013. In August of that year, when his partner tried to end their relationship, he killed his son Logan, who was 21 months old.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of serial killer Geoffrey Evans and his accomplice John Shaw, who hit headlines in 1976 after the murders of two women in Ireland.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: She became the first woman to be executed by lethal injection after taking six lives. What drove her to commit these callous murders?

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A profile into the murderer who terrorised the gay community as the 'Trash Bag Killer' and confessed to killing 32 victims.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Robert Hansen was a monster who preyed on the most vulnerable women in society - dehumanising them for his own twisted fantasies. Stranded with their killer in the vast Alaskan wilderness his victims had no chance of survival.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of 27-year-old Pawel Relowicz, who raped and killed 21-year-old Hull student Libby Squire in a case that made headlines around the UK.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Charles Albright's entire life revolved around crime. He graduated from being a small-time crook, into a child-molester and then evolved into a serial killer.
